title: Drone Flight Over Stunning Kenyan Landscape: Exploring Nature's Beauty
creator: PowerTale Productions (@PowertaleProductions)
notes: SAVANNA/GRASSLAND-LANDSCAPE EXEMPLAR (subgenre: savanna and grassland sunset landscapes, pure landscape framing rather than an animal/wildlife focus) -- added following the same per-record verification rigor as this pack's original 10 entries (see e.g. bbc-earth-aerial.json, skypixel-landscape.json). Independently re-verified this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/mais1F_tZI8&format=json), which returned: title 'Drone Flight Over Stunning Kenyan Landscape: Exploring Nature's Beauty', author_name 'PowerTale Productions', author_url 'https://www.youtube.com/@PowertaleProductions', and embed dimensions width=113/height=200 (a vertical, portrait-oriented aspect ratio) -- this matches the prior agent's candidate record field-for-field (same title, same author_name) and the width<height embed geometry independently corroborates the '/shorts/' URL path's short-form/vertical-video claim, rather than relying on the URL slug alone. A separate WebFetch attempt against the live https://www.youtube.com/shorts/mais1F_tZI8 page in this session returned only truncated/JS-rendered content (no description text, no license marking, no upload date), the same bot-check limitation the prior agent's candidate JSON already flagged -- so, as with that candidate, the savanna/grassland sunset framing and the absence of an animal/wildlife focus are inferred from the title's wording only ('Kenyan Landscape', 'Nature's Beauty') and have NOT been independently confirmed by watching or scrubbing the footage in this session. This record is still included, at medium confidence, because it is the only candidate researched for this pack that targets the savanna/grassland-landscape subgenre specifically (as distinct from the pack's existing wildlife-focused African exemplars, e.g. skypixel11_africa_unseen_ellisvanjason.json, natgeo-aerial-wildlife.json, drone-photography-awards-wildlife.json), and its notes are explicit about the limits of what was actually verified rather than presenting inference as observation. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved' because no explicit Creative Commons/CC0 marking was found anywhere in this session's oEmbed or WebFetch responses (per this project's stated default: assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response for Shorts does not include a duration field, and the blocked full-page fetch prevented any further runtime confirmation in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number (e.g. assuming a typical ~15-60s Shorts length), this field is left unset pending a real YouTube Data API call or manual playback confirmation. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: no festival, contest, or broadcaster showcase selection was found or claimed anywhere in this session's research for this smaller/independent creator channel -- inventing one would violate the no-fabrication constraint. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, deliberately placed at the lower-middle of this pack's existing 0.70-0.88 range given medium confidence and an independent/small creator with no verified contest or broadcaster pedigree (comparable to this pack's other lower-scoring independent-creator entries, e.g. backman-family-monteverde-cloud-forest.json at 0.7133, wanderpeaks-langhe-autumn-vineyards.json at 0.7233): sharpness=0.72 (no drone/camera platform was confirmed in this session, and Shorts-format re-compression measurably degrades perceived per-frame detail relative to a broadcaster/festival master file, so scored at this pack's lower-middle rather than assuming a high-end platform); exposure=0.71 (the title implies landscape/golden-hour-adjacent conditions but this was not independently confirmed by playback, and unconfirmed backlit/sunset scenes are a harder dynamic-range case than flat daylight, so scored conservatively near this pack's lower end rather than assuming well-managed exposure); motion_smoothness=0.72 (a vertical Shorts-format edit plausibly assembled from multiple short flight segments rather than one continuous cinematic take, with no gimbal/stabilization claim verified in this session, so scored at this pack's lower-middle rather than at its high end). `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.72, exposure=0.71, motion_smoothness=0.72, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` via `.venv/bin/python3 -c "..."` in this session, returning 0.7166666666666667 (weights_version 'default-v2'; since `composition` is None, `compute_composite_score` renormalizes over only the three populated equal-weighted signals, so the result is an equal 1/3-each average of the three inputs regardless of the weights profile's raw per-signal values) -- rounded to 0.7167 here. `editorial_style` reflects a genuine attempt, not a shortcut: the oEmbed response's embed dimensions (width=113/height=200) confirm a vertical/portrait video, consistent with a Shorts-format edit, but no cut count, shot length, transition style, or pacing description was available from either the oEmbed response or the blocked WebFetch attempt in this session, and no browser-automation/playback tool (e.g. claude-in-chrome) was invoked to scrub or watch the actual video -- so `review_method` stays 'not_reviewed' and all quantitative editorial fields (format, estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, transition_styles_observed) stay null/empty rather than being guessed from the video's likely genre.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Live playback review was not performed in this session: no browser-automation/video playback tool (e.g. claude-in-chrome) was invoked to scrub or watch this Short. YouTube's oEmbed endpoint confirmed the title, author, and vertical embed geometry (width=113/height=200) but returned no description, caption, or timing metadata. A separate WebFetch of the live https://www.youtube.com/shorts/mais1F_tZI8 page returned only truncated/JS-rendered content with no cut count, shot length, transition style, or pacing description of any kind. Given the '/shorts/' URL path and the confirmed vertical aspect ratio, a short-form/punchy edit is plausible, but this is an inference from platform/URL convention and embed geometry, not an observation, so `format` is left null rather than asserted as 'short-form-reel' without an actual review.
